mission.title: cloud-native-network: Make failure message great again
mission.description: Make failure message great again. Requested by 1+ users.

steps.title: Check current cloud-native-network deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cloud-native-network version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
helm list -n cloud-native-network 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cloud-native-network installation.

steps.title: Review cloud-native-network configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cloud-native-network configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
kubectl get configmap -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cloud-native-network
```
Right now if we had a test failed when run `cargo test`, the failure message is missing and we can not locate the assert that failed directly.  

**And if comment the tests code in `tty.rs`, the failure message show up again.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make failure message great again
steps.description:
Fix #185

This PR replaces clone(2) with double fork to create the container process. Double fork was the original implementation, and we wisely thought that we could improve it by using a single clone call. Turns out jokes on us that we can't use a single clone call. With clone, if we enter into a new pid namespace, we are OK. However, if we need to set_ns into an existing pid namespace, we are out of luck. So the right path forward needs to be double fork. For a more detailed discussion, see

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
kubectl get events -n cloud-native-network --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Make failure message great again" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fix #185

This PR replaces clone(2) with double fork to create the container process. Double fork was the original implementation, and we wisely thought that we could improve it by using a single clone call. Turns out jokes on us that we can't use a single clone call. With clone, if we enter into a new pid namespace, we are OK.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cloud-native-network installed or the issue environment reproducible.
